## Summary
Inflationary gravitational waves would imprint a distinctive B-mode polarization pattern on the CMB. Detection would confirm inflation and constrain the energy scale. Current upper bounds place r < 0.036 (95% CL).

## Evidence
BICEP/Keck 2021 data combined with Planck yield r < 0.036. CMB-S4 and LiteBIRD aim for σ(r) ~ 0.001, probing most large-field inflation models. Galactic foreground subtraction remains the dominant challenge.

## Significance
A detection of primordial gravitational waves would be a smoking gun for inflation and measure the energy scale directly. The null result from current experiments already rules out the simplest φ² chaotic inflation models.
